name: Auto-Refund Policy Simulator
description: Models the behavioral impact of enabling AI-powered automatic refunds. Tracks how trust, abuse rates, and revenue interact over time under different assumptions.
thesis: Enabling AI auto-refunds under $100 creates a feedback loop: easy refunds attract abuse, abuse erodes trust, trust erosion drives more escalations, and escalations consume the margin the automation was supposed to save. The question is not IF this happens — but WHEN, and under what conditions it becomes irreversible.
